stratification.note: Stratification type undeterminable from available clinical input — physician selection required

validated.stg_reasoning: The patient satisfies multiple clinical indications for PCNL. Right-sided colicky flank pain and positive right costovertebral angle tenderness satisfy the 'Flank pain' indication. Intermittent gross haematuria and urine routine showing RBCs 12-15/hpf satisfy the 'Hematuria' indication. Most importantly, the NCCT KUB reveals a 2.5 cm (25 mm) staghorn calculus in the right renal pelvis, which directly satisfies the 'Presence of renal stone >20 mm' indication. This finding also unambiguously meets the mandatory clinical threshold of 'renal_stone_size > 20 mm' as confirmed by NCCT KUB. All required criteria are clearly and explicitly met by the patient's clinical presentation and investigation findings.
